content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 11, 1989

Isabel Sewell
1049 Noel Dr. #1
Menlo Park, CA 94025

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-89-449

Dear Ms. Sewell:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act of 1974.¹ Your questions are of a
general nature; therefore, we consider your request to be for
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy
enclosed).²

QUESTION

May a non-profit organization pay for material, such as
brochures, to support a bond measure without organizing a
committee?

CONCLUSION

If your organization receives $1,000 in contributions during
a calendar year for political purposes, including supporting the
passage of a ballot measure, it must file and report as a
committee. In addition, if your organization makes independent
expenditures totaling $1,000 during a calendar year to support or
oppose candidates or ballot measures, or makes contributions
totaling $10,000 in a calendar year to support or oppose
candidates or ballot measures, your organization will have
reporting obligations.

To determine to what extent a non-profit organization may
engage in political practices while maintaining its tax status,
please contact the IRS at 800/424-1040.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Isabel Sewell
Page Two

FACTS

Friends of the Library ("Friends") exists to enhance library
functions. Friends would like to support a local bond measure by
paying for a brochure and its mailing. Friends is a non-profit
organization.

ANALYSIS

The Political Reform Act places certain reporting obligations
on committees. Section 82013 defines a committee as any person or
combination of persons who: (a) receives contributions totaling
$1,000 or more in a calendar year, (b) makes independent
expenditures totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year; or (c)
makes contributions totaling $10,000 or more in a calendar year to
or at the behest of candidates or committees.

Section 82015 defines a "contribution" as:

...a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a loan by
a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a payment
except to the extent that full and adequate consideration is
received unless it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political purposes.

Attempting to influence the action of the voters for passage
of a ballot measure is included in the definition of a "political
purpose." (Regulation 18215.)

Section 82031 defines an "independent expenditure" as:

...an expenditure made by any person in connection with a
communication which expressly advocates the election or
defeat of a clearly identified candidate or the
qualification, passage or defeat of a clearly identified
measure, or taken as a whole and in context, unambiguously
urges a particular result in an election but which is not
made to or at the behest of the affected candidate or
committee. (Emphasis added.)

A payment is "made at the behest" of a candidate or committee
if the payment is made under the control or at the direction of,
in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with, or at
the request or suggestion of a candidate or committee.
(Regulation 18215(b).)

Therefore, if the organization receives contributions
totaling $1,000 or more to publish the brochure, it will qualify
as a "committee" under Section 82013(a). If the organization uses
funds which are not contributions to the organization to publish
the brochure, it may qualify as a "committee" under Section
82013(b) or (c). Enclosed is a copy of the 1989 "Information
Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform
Act" which explains the Act's disclosure requirements.

Isabel Sewell
Page Three

     If you have any additional questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
                                             Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

Fair Political Practises Committee
428 J St
Sacto CA 95814

FPPC

JUL 26 2 [illegible] 989

Dear Sir/Madam:

I would like to have a copy of your information manual about non-profits
supporting a campaign for a city bond measure. I am not sure if this would
require a financial statement from the non profit, and under what conditions
we could pay for a brochure to be mailed to citizens, without having to
actually organize a campaign. The organization supplying the money would
be the Friends of the Library, whose sole purpose is to enhance library
functions My question is Can we pay for material to gain support for the
bond measure without organizing a separate campaign?

Thanks for your help,

Isabel Sewell
1049 Noel Dr # 1
Menlo Park CA 94025

824-449

Fair Political Practises Committee
428 J St
Sacto CA 95814

FPPC
JUL 26 2 ఈм:Ӏ29ԻԻ 1989

Dear Sir/Madam:

I would like to have a copy of your information manual about non-profits
supporting a campaign for a city bond measure. I am not sure if this would
require a financial statement from the non profit, and under what conditions
we could pay for a brochure to be mailed to citizens, without having to
actually organize a campaign. The organization supplying the money would
be the Friends of the Library, whose sole purpose is to enhance library
functions. My question is Can we pay for material to gain support for the
bond measure without organizing a separate campaign?

Thanks for your help,
[signature]
Isabel Sewell
1049 Noel Dr # 1
Menlo Park CA 94025

July 28, 1989


Isabel Sewell
1049 Noel Drive, #1
Menlo Park, CA  94025

                                        Re:  Letter No. 89-449

Dear Ms. Sewell:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on July 27, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 11
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        [illegible]
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Chief Technical Assistance
                                          and Analysis Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
